title: [EPIC]: Release 2 Codebase Refactor — Establish a clean, typed, tested, and pattern-driven foundation
label: epic
summary: Refactor the existing MVP codebase into a clean, efficient Release 2 that follows established design patterns, enforces type safety through interfaces and contracts, eliminates duplication via DRY principles, and introduces meaningful test coverage. R2 becomes the authoritative reference point in this application's SDLC.
problem_hl: The current codebase was built incrementally by humans and chatbots during MVP development, resulting in duplicated logic, inconsistent patterns, weak typing (widespread use of 'any'), no automated tests, missing linting/formatting, oversized monolithic components, scattered hardcoded values, and debug logging left in production paths. This makes the code fragile, hard to reason about, and expensive to extend — an unreliable foundation for future releases.
goals: Eliminate all DRY violations: extract shared hooks (useStages, useSubStages), unify the API fetch layer, and centralise constants (default colours, seed data, error messages)
Achieve full type safety: remove every 'any' annotation, define explicit interfaces/contracts for storage, API request/response shapes, component props, and domain entities
Decompose oversized components (Admin 595 LOC, Dashboard 473 LOC, KanbanBoard 394 LOC, EditTaskDialog 387 LOC) into focused, single-responsibility modules under 200 LOC each

Introduce automated test coverage: unit tests for hooks, utility functions, and server route handlers; integration tests for the storage layer; component tests for critical UI flows — targeting ≥70% line coverage
Establish and enforce code quality tooling: ESLint with strict TypeScript rules, Prettier for formatting, Husky pre-commit hooks, and CI-ready npm scripts (lint, format, test, check)
Standardise error handling: server-side middleware for consistent error responses with proper HTTP status codes; client-side error boundary and toast-based user feedback
Replace all console.log debug statements with a configurable structured logger (respects NODE_ENV)
Produce a codebase that serves as the canonical reference architecture for all subsequent releases
scope: Phase 1: Tooling & guardrails — Add ESLint (strict TS), Prettier, lint-staged + Husky pre-commit, npm scripts (lint, format, test, check). Fix all new linter errors introduced. Configure Vitest with coverage reporting.

Phase 2: Type safety & contracts — Remove every 'any' type. Define and export interfaces for Stage, SubStage, Task, InsertTask, API responses, storage methods, and component props. Enforce strict typing in tsconfig (noImplicitAny already on — verify and harden).

Phase 3: DRY extraction & shared modules — Create useStages and useSubStages hooks to replace 7+ inline useQuery duplicates. Unify all fetch calls behind a single apiRequest wrapper with typed generics. Extract default colours, seed data, error messages, and magic strings into a shared constants module. Create a parseIdParam helper for the 11+ repeated parseInt/isNaN blocks in server routes.

Phase 4: Component decomposition — Split Admin.tsx into StageManager, SubStageManager, and AdminLayout. Split Dashboard.tsx into DashboardToolbar, DashboardContent, and view-mode subcomponents. Split KanbanBoard.tsx into BoardLayout, DragController, and column orchestration. Split EditTaskDialog.tsx into form section subcomponents. Ensure each resulting module is ≤200 LOC.

Phase 5: Error handling & logging — Implement Express error-handling middleware with typed error classes and consistent JSON responses. Add a React ErrorBoundary at the app root. Replace all console.log/console.error with a lightweight logger (e.g. pino or custom) gated by NODE_ENV. Audit and fix the rethrow-after-response bug in server/index.ts error handler.

Phase 6: Test coverage — Write Vitest unit tests for all extracted hooks, utility functions, and shared modules. Write integration tests for DatabaseStorage CRUD operations. Write component tests for TaskCard, CreateTaskDialog, EditTaskDialog rendering and validation. Write API route handler tests using supertest. Achieve ≥70% line coverage.

Phase 7: Cleanup & documentation — Remove unused dependencies (passport, express-session, ws if unused). Remove or gate Replit-specific plugins behind environment checks. Update ARCHITECTURE.md, COMPONENT_INDEX.md, and API_REFERENCE.md to reflect R2 structure. Tag the release as v2.0.0.
out_of_scope: New user-facing features — R2 is purely structural; no new UI capabilities, screens, or workflows
Database schema changes — the existing Drizzle schema and migrations remain unchanged
Migration to a different framework, ORM, or state management library
Performance optimisation (bundle splitting, lazy loading, caching) — deferred to a dedicated performance epic
CI/CD pipeline setup — tooling is CI-ready but pipeline configuration is a separate concern
Visual/design refresh — styling and theming remain as-is
acceptance: Zero 'any' types in the codebase (verified by tsc --strict with noImplicitAny)
ESLint passes with zero errors on the full codebase (npm run lint exits 0)
Prettier reports zero formatting violations (npm run format --check exits 0)
Vitest test suite passes with ≥70% line coverage (npm run test exits 0)
No component file exceeds 200 lines of code (excluding UI primitives in components/ui/)
All stage/sub-stage data fetching uses shared hooks (useStages, useSubStages) — no inline useQuery for these resources outside the hooks
All API calls route through a single typed apiRequest wrapper
Zero console.log or console.error statements outside the logger module
Server error handler returns consistent JSON shape {error, message, status} for all 4xx/5xx responses
A React ErrorBoundary catches and renders fallback UI for unhandled client errors
ARCHITECTURE.md and COMPONENT_INDEX.md accurately reflect the R2 module structure
Repository is tagged v2.0.0 with a changelog summarising all refactoring work
